graphifyNote: graphify-out/graph.json is present; Layer 2 taint-flow analysis is graph-informed. The source has zero HTML sinks, zero eval, and zero user-input flows, so the graph confirms rather than changes the clean Layer 2 result.

changeFromPriorRun.note: Per-layer code baseline unchanged (already clean). The four supplemental repo-hardening gaps that were 'missing' in the prior run are now closed; a real high-severity tmp advisory was caught and fixed; this run is graph-enriched (prior was noGraphify). CSP + five response headers remain 'missing' (no deploy target).
closed: supplemental.tmp-advisory: introduced fix -> tmp '>=0.2.6' override in pnpm-workspace.yaml (GHSA-ph9p-34f9-6g65)
changeFromPriorRun.stillOpenRootCause: No deployment target. CSP and response security headers must be set at the deployment edge or in a framework headers() function; neither exists for this local-only template. Graded 'missing' (not 'violation') per the baseline.

supplementalBaselineGaps.note: Hardening practices the prompt asked to grade that sit alongside but outside the four-layer per-check baseline. Reported here so they are visible without inflating the layer tallies. All four headline items closed since the prior run.

items.evidence: .github/workflows/codeql.yml runs github/codeql-action init+analyze for javascript-typescript on push (main), pull_request, and a weekly cron (0 6 * * 1). Least-privilege permissions (contents: read, security-events: write); cancel-in-progress concurrency.
.github/dependabot.yml configures npm and github-actions ecosystems on a weekly schedule; dev dependencies grouped into one PR; open-pull-requests-limit 10.
SECURITY.md documents a private vulnerability-disclosure path (GitHub private advisory + email ben@codewalnut.com), a supported-versions note, and a summary of the automated controls.

scripts/secret-scan.sh runs 'gitleaks protect --staged --redact' and is invoked from .husky/pre-commit. Degrades gracefully (install hint, exit 0) when gitleaks is absent locally; CI runs gitleaks over full history as the hard backstop. Closes the 'Missing Staged-Content Secret Scan' gap from the quality-gates run.

A real high-severity transitive advisory, tmp < 0.2.6 (GHSA-ph9p-34f9-6g65, path traversal) pulled via @lhci/cli -> inquirer -> external-editor, was caught by the CI 'pnpm audit --audit-level=high' gate and fixed by 'overrides: tmp: ">=0.2.6"' in pnpm-workspace.yaml, pinned across the whole tree.

Linter is Biome; ESLint and the eslint-plugin-security / eslint-plugin-no-unsanitized / eslint-plugin-react-security family are absent, so '--with-scan' would find no recognised scanners. CodeQL now provides the SAST coverage; an optional ESLint regression guard is recommended.

evidence: No localStorage/sessionStorage usage anywhere in src/. No auth library in dependencies. The app has no session concept.
checks.expectation: Long-lived session/auth tokens live in HttpOnly cookies, not localStorage/sessionStorage.
checks.gap: None — there are no tokens and no storage.
checks.remediation: No action. Re-run this check if/when authentication is introduced.

evidence: No auth flow, no logout, no server.
checks.expectation: Logout calls a server endpoint, not just clearing local state.

evidence: No auth state; no network calls in src/.
checks.expectation: Auth state resolves against a server source of truth on navigation/load.

evidence: No OAuth flow detected (skipped silently per baseline).
checks.expectation: Public clients use PKCE.

evidence: No OAuth flow detected.
checks.expectation: Every OAuth authorization request includes a validated state parameter.

evidence: No OIDC flow detected (skipped silently per baseline).
checks.expectation: OIDC flows include a validated nonce.

evidence: No router, no ?next=/?returnTo= reflection, no router.push/window.location from input.
checks.expectation: Post-auth redirect destinations are validated against an allowlist or restricted to relative paths.

evidence: No role/permission model exists. Recorded partial only to honour the soft-check vocabulary; there is no authorization surface to gate.
checks.expectation: UI-only authorization checks are paired with server-enforced checks at the data-fetching layer.
checks.remediation: No action until auth is added.

evidence: 0 occurrences of dangerouslySetInnerHTML across src/ and index.html.
checks.expectation: Every dangerouslySetInnerHTML is sanitized or from a trusted server-rendered source.

evidence: 0 occurrences of eval, new Function, Function(...), or string-form setTimeout/setInterval.
checks.expectation: No dynamic code execution in source.

evidence: 0 raw innerHTML/outerHTML assignments. All DOM mutation goes through React.
checks.expectation: DOM mutations go through React, not element.innerHTML = userInput.

evidence: No user-controlled href/src/formAction expressions. The one <script src> in index.html:10 is a first-party Vite module literal.
checks.expectation: User-controlled URLs in href/src/formAction allow only http/https/relative.

evidence: No redirect call sites (no router.push, window.location.assign, or location.href= from input).
checks.expectation: Redirects do not pass user-controlled URLs through unchecked.

evidence: No Markdown/rich-text rendering library (no react-markdown, marked, remark/rehype, draft-js, slate, tiptap, quill).
checks.expectation: Markdown/rich-text uses a library that disallows HTML by default or sanitizes after.

evidence: Standard @vitejs/plugin-react JSX runtime; no raw-HTML React.createElement, no escaping-disabled custom runtime, no render-unsafe components.
checks.expectation: No bypass of React's auto-escaping.

evidence: No deployment config; per baseline this is partial (not violation) for local-only projects. The only http:// literal (playwright.config.ts:12) is a localhost test default, not shipped.
checks.expectation: Deployed app redirects HTTP to HTTPS at the edge, or carries HSTS.
checks.gap: No HTTPS enforcement configured (no deploy target).
checks.remediation: Enforce HTTPS at the deployment edge once a host is chosen.

evidence: No HSTS header source anywhere (no deployment config).
checks.expectation: HSTS header with max-age >= 15768000; includeSubDomains recommended.
checks.remediation: Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload at the deployment edge once a host is chosen.

evidence: No CSP via response header (no deployment config) and no <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"> in index.html (index.html:1-12). This is the single highest-leverage missing control.
checks.expectation: A CSP is set via response header or <meta http-equiv>.
checks.remediation: Cheapest in-repo stopgap: a <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"> in index.html with default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'. Promote to a response header at the deployment edge for full coverage (meta cannot carry frame-ancestors or HSTS).

evidence: No CSP exists to evaluate for permissiveness.
checks.expectation: CSP avoids unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval for script-src and does not use * as a source.
checks.remediation: When adding the CSP, keep script-src to 'self' with no unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval.

evidence: No CSP frame-ancestors and no X-Frame-Options anywhere. The app can be framed by any origin — clickjacking is undefended.
checks.expectation: CSP frame-ancestors set, or X-Frame-Options DENY/SAMEORIGIN.
checks.remediation: Add frame-ancestors 'none' to the CSP (header form) or X-Frame-Options: DENY at the edge.

evidence: No X-Content-Type-Options header anywhere.
checks.expectation: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is configured.
checks.remediation: Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff at the deployment edge.

evidence: No Referrer-Policy header anywhere.
checks.expectation: A non-default Referrer-Policy (strict-origin-when-cross-origin or stricter).
checks.remediation: Add Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin at the deployment edge (or a <meta name="referrer"> tag as a stopgap).

evidence: No Permissions-Policy header anywhere.
checks.expectation: A Permissions-Policy restricts dangerous features (geolocation, camera, microphone, payment, USB).
checks.remediation: Add Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), camera=(), microphone=(), payment=(), usb=() at the deployment edge.

evidence: No cookies are set anywhere (no document.cookie, no cookie helper, no server adapter).
checks.expectation: Auth/session cookies carry Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax or stricter.

evidence: No http:// asset/fetch URLs in production source. The only http:// literal is a localhost test default in playwright.config.ts:12.
checks.expectation: Asset and fetch URLs use https:// or relative paths.

evidence: 0 secret-format regex matches across tracked source. Defended at two layers now: gitleaks protect --staged on pre-commit (scripts/secret-scan.sh via .husky/pre-commit) and gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v3 over full history in CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml security job).
checks.expectation: No API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys, or signing secrets as string literals.

evidence: .gitignore:27-29 ignores .env and .env.* and allowlists !.env.example. Only .env.example is tracked (git ls-files), and it documents a single non-secret CI knob (E2E_BASE_URL).
checks.expectation: .gitignore excludes .env, .env.local, .env.*.local.

evidence: No VITE_*/NEXT_PUBLIC_*/PUBLIC_* variables exist. The only process.env reads are CI/test-time (process.env.CI, E2E_BASE_URL, STYLEMAP_DIR) in playwright.config.ts and e2e/styleproof.spec.ts — never bundled into client code.
checks.expectation: Public-prefixed vars do not carry secret content.

evidence: No router or URL parameters exist at all; nothing matches PII patterns. Reported partial per the soft-check vocabulary; no real exposure.
checks.expectation: No email/phone/government-ID/token parameters in URLs.
checks.remediation: No action; re-check if routing with parameters is added.

evidence: No analytics library (no gtag, posthog, mixpanel, segment, amplitude, heap).
checks.expectation: Analytics event payloads do not include PII fields.

evidence: 0 external scripts. The one <script> in index.html:10 is a first-party Vite module; SRI does not apply to bundler-managed first-party modules.
checks.expectation: External non-first-party <script> tags carry an integrity attribute.

evidence: 0 <iframe> elements.
checks.expectation: Third-party iframes use the sandbox attribute.

evidence: 0 window message handlers / postMessage usage.
checks.expectation: message handlers check event.origin against an allowlist before acting on event.data.

evidence: 0 target="_blank" links.
checks.expectation: target="_blank" links include rel="noopener noreferrer".

evidence: 0 console.* calls in src/ (and none in e2e/). No tokens, env values, or header objects logged.
checks.expectation: No auth tokens, env values, or request-header objects passed to console.* or loggers.

evidence: No crypto code; no MD5 or SHA-1 usage in source.
checks.expectation: No MD5/SHA-1 for security-relevant purposes.
